  • index
  • next |
  • previous |
  • Minerva Schema 5.0.0 documentation »
  • Schema Reference »

trend_directory¶

Stores information with fixed interval and format, like periodic measurements.

Tables¶

materialization_trend_store_link¶

Stores the dependencies between a materialization and its source table trend stores. Multiple levels of views and functions may exist between a materialization and its source table trend stores. These intermediate views and functions are not registered here, but only the table trend stores containing the actual source data used in the views and/or functions.

Name Type Description
materialization_id int4  
trend_store_id int4  

table_trend_store¶

Name Type Description
name name  
entity_type_id int4  
data_source_id int4  
granularity interval  
partition_size int4  
id int4  
retention_period interval  

trend¶

Name Type Description
trend_store_id int4  
name name  
data_type text  
description text  
id int4  

view_trend¶

Name Type Description
trend_store_id int4  
name name  
data_type text  
description text  
id int4  

partition¶

Name Type Description
table_trend_store_id int4  
index int4  

trend_tag_link¶

Name Type Description
trend_id int4  
tag_id int4  

modified¶

Name Type Description
table_trend_store_id int4  
timestamp timestamptz  
start timestamptz  
end timestamptz  

materialization¶

Indicates if jobs should be created for this materialization (manual execution is always possible)

Name Type Description
dst_trend_store_id int4 The ID of the destination table_trend_store
processing_delay interval The time after the destination timestamp before this materialization can be executed
stability_delay interval The time to wait after the most recent modified timestamp before the source data is considered ‘stable’
reprocessing_period interval The maximum time after the destination timestamp that the materialization is allowed to be executed
id int4 The unique identifier of this materialization
enabled bool Indicates if jobs should be created for this materialization (manual execution is always possible)
cost int4  

view_materialization¶

A table_materialization is a materialization that uses the data from the function registered in the src_function column to populate the target trend store.

Name Type Description
dst_trend_store_id int4  
processing_delay interval  
stability_delay interval  
reprocessing_period interval  
src_view regclass  
id int4  
enabled bool  
cost int4  

function_materialization¶

Name Type Description
dst_trend_store_id int4  
processing_delay interval  
stability_delay interval  
reprocessing_period interval  
src_function regprocedure  
id int4  
enabled bool  
cost int4  

state¶

ID of the most recent job for this materialization

Name Type Description
materialization_id int4 The ID of the materialization type
timestamp timestamptz The timestamp of the materialized (materialization result) data
max_modified timestamptz The greatest modified timestamp of all materialization sources
source_states _source_fragment_state Array of trend_store_id/timestamp/modified combinations for all source data fragments
processed_states _source_fragment_state Array containing a snapshot of the source_states at the time of the most recent materialization
job_id int4 ID of the most recent job for this materialization

materialization_tag_link¶

Links tags to materializations. Examples of tags to link to a materialization might be: online, offline, aggregation, kpi, etc.

Name Type Description
materialization_id int4  
tag_id int4  

group_priority¶

Name Type Description
tag_id int4  
resources int4  

trend_store¶

Name Type Description
name name  
entity_type_id int4  
data_source_id int4  
granularity interval  
id int4  

view_trend_store¶

Name Type Description
name name  
entity_type_id int4  
data_source_id int4  
granularity interval  
id int4  

table_trend¶

Name Type Description
trend_store_id int4  
name name  
data_type text  
description text  
id int4

update_modified(integer, timestamp with time zone, timestamp with time zone) -> trend_directory.modified¶

store_modified(integer, timestamp with time zone, timestamp with time zone) -> trend_directory.modified¶

mark_modified(integer, timestamp with time zone, timestamp with time zone) -> trend_directory.modified¶

populate_modified(trend_directory.partition) -> SETOF trend_directory.modified¶

Populate trend_directory.modified table with modified records from one partition. This function should only be used in recovery scenarios where the trend_directory.modified table has become corrupt or records are missing for some reason.

populate_modified(trend_directory.table_trend_store) -> SETOF trend_directory.modified¶

Populate trend_directory.modified table with modified records from a whole trend store. This function should only be used in recovery scenarios where the trend_directory.modified table has become corrupt or records are missing for some reason.

available_timestamps(trend_directory.partition) -> SETOF timestamp with time zone¶

transfer(trend_directory.trend_store, trend_directory.trend_store, timestamp with time zone, text[]) -> trend_directory.transfer_result¶

tag(text, integer) -> trend_directory.materialization_tag_link¶

Add tag with name tag_name to materialization with id materialization_id. The tag must already exist.

tag(text, trend_directory.materialization) -> trend_directory.materialization¶

Add tag with name tag_name to materialization. The tag must already exist.

untag(trend_directory.materialization) -> trend_directory.materialization¶

Remove all tags from the materialization

reset(integer) -> SETOF trend_directory.state¶

reset_hard(trend_directory.materialization) -> void¶

Remove data (partitions) resulting from this materialization and the corresponding state records, so materialization for all timestamps can be done again

reset(trend_directory.materialization, timestamp with time zone) -> trend_directory.state¶

disable(trend_directory.materialization) -> trend_directory.materialization¶
